Tests are now failing when i run `update.sh` locally 
the build action works but test action fails on github as well 
test-unit
Process completed with exit code 1.
[angulareighteen] src/app/app.spec.ts: src/app/app.spec.ts#L3
Error: Playwright Test did not expect test() to be called here.
Most common reasons include:
- You are calling test() in a configuration file.
- You are calling test() in a file that is imported by the configuration file.
- You have two different versions of @playwright/test. This usually happens
  when one of the dependencies in your package.json depends on @playwright/test.
- You are calling test() from an async test.describe() block. Only sync ones are supported.
 ❯ _TestTypeImpl._currentSuite node_modules/playwright/lib/common/index.js:2257:13
 ❯ _TestTypeImpl._createTest node_modules/playwright/lib/common/index.js:2271:24
 ❯ node_modules/playwright/lib/common/index.js:1220:12
 ❯ src/app/app.spec.ts:3:1
 ❯ src/app/app.spec.ts:1:1

Error: Playwright Test did not expect test() to be called here.
Most common reasons include:
- You are calling test() in a configuration file.
- You are calling test() in a file that is imported by the configuration file.
- You have two different versions of @playwright/test. This usually happens
  when one of the dependencies in your package.json depends on @playwright/test.
- You are calling test() from an async test.describe() block. Only sync ones are supported.

Identified misplaced Playwright test in unit test directory
Identified misplaced Playwright test in unit test directory
Looking at the test failure, there's a Playwright configuration issue where test() is being called in a spec file when it shouldn't be—likely because the test file is being imported by the Playwright config or there's a version mismatch with @playwright/test in the dependencies. The issue is that src/app/app.spec.ts is a Vitest unit test file, but somehow the test() function is resolving to Playwright's test instead of Vitest's, which suggests there's a module resolution conflict. I should examine the uploaded files to see what's actually being imported and how the test configuration is set up. playwright/test'`, then that's the problem — the spec file is importing Playwright's test instead of Vitest's. The error makes sense now: Playwright's test function is being invoked, which tries to use Playwright's test runner infrastructure.

The real issue might be that both Vitest and Playwright are configured to run the same spec files, or the spec file has the wrong import statement pulling in Playwright instead of Vitest.

Let me look at the actual project files to see what's happening — I need to check the configuration and the spec file itself to understand where the conflict is coming from.

Reading test output log
I can see the failing test file is a Playwright e2e test that's checking whether the home page displays the quiz and news links properly.

The issue is that this Playwright test is sitting in src/app/ with a .spec.ts extension, which means Vitest's unit test runner is picking it up and trying to execute it. But Playwright's test() function can't run in the Vitest context, causing the failure. The root problem is a misplaced e2e test living inside the unit test glob.

To fix this, I need to understand the intended structure by checking the configuration files—specifically where e2e tests should live, how Vitest's include glob is set up, the Playwright configuration, and the Angular build target settings. I need to dig into the actual files to understand the full picture—checking the app.spec.ts content itself, where Playwright's testDir is configured, whether there's already a proper unit test for the App component, and how the build is set up for unit tests versus e2e tests.

Identified duplicate Playwright test file causing Vitest collision

Comparing e2e/app.spec.ts vs src/app/app.spec.ts

Script
I've found that src/app/app.spec.ts is an exact duplicate of the e2e test file, meaning it's a Playwright test that's mistakenly placed in the unit test directory instead of where it belongs.

The git history shows the e2e version was last touched in an earlier commit, while the src version was modified more recently today, so the duplicate in src is the problem. I need to delete it since the proper e2e test already exists at e2e/app.spec.ts.

Before removing it though, I should verify the test configuration — checking what the Vitest glob pattern includes, whether Playwright's testDir is properly scoped to just the e2e folder, and confirming the npm scripts are set up correctly so that removing this file won't break anything else.

The real fix is straightforward: delete src/app/app.spec.ts via git rm. But I should also consider hardening the config to prevent this from happening again — either by making the unit test builder explicitly exclude e2e patterns, or by adjusting the tsconfig.spec.json to be more restrictive about what it includes.

Let me check the current configuration in angular.json, tsconfig.spec.json, and the test setup to see what's the minimal change needed.

Script
I'm looking at the Angular test configuration setup, where the test target uses Vitest as the test runner and references a separate TypeScript config file that extends the main config while adding Vitest globals and specifying the spec output directory.

The key insight is that the test discovery mechanism picks up files matching the include pattern in tsconfig.spec.json, which is why src/app/app.spec.ts gets picked up as a unit test even though it's actually a Playwright test file that belongs in the e2e/ directory. The minimal fix is to remove that stray duplicate file using git rm, but I should also consider adding a defense-in-depth configuration to prevent this kind of accident in the future. Done
The unit-test builder discovers specs via tsconfig.spec.json's include: ["src/**/*.spec.ts"], so the stray src/app/app.spec.ts gets pulled into Vitest. Let me check the rest of the config and confirm there are no other misplaced Playwright tests.

I'm seeing the test file structure now — the unit test in src/app/app.component.spec.ts is properly set up with Vitest, and Playwright is correctly configured to only look in the e2e/ directory where the legitimate e2e test lives. But there's a duplicate copy of that e2e test sitting in src/app/app.spec.ts that shouldn't be there.

Since tsconfig.spec.json picks up everything matching src/**/*.spec.ts, Vitest is trying to run this Playwright test file and failing because Playwright's test() function isn't compatible with Vitest. The solution is straightforward: delete src/app/app.spec.ts entirely, since the proper e2e coverage already exists at e2e/app.spec.ts. This was likely an accidental copy-paste or merge artifact. So src/app/app.spec.ts is a complete duplicate of the e2e version and should be deleted using git rm since it's tracked in version control. The file was committed in the "fix defects" commit, so I need to remove it properly from git rather than just deleting it locally. There's a timestamp mismatch here—the dump header says it was generated at 09:23, but the file metadata shows 13:18, which is later. This could mean the user regenerated the dump after the initial one, or the header timestamp wasn't updated. Either way, it's a minor inconsistency that doesn't affect the actual fix.

The real issue is clear: src/app/app.spec.ts exists in git, it's a Playwright test file, and it's breaking ng test. It's a duplicate of the one in the e2e directory. The solution is straightforward—delete it using git rm src/app/app.spec.ts, and that should resolve the failing unit test both locally and in CI. So src/app/app.spec.ts is a 100% redundant, misplaced copy of e2e/app.spec.ts that Vitest wrongly executes.

The CI test job runs the same Angular test command that picks up that stray spec file, so deleting src/app/app.spec.ts will fix both the unit test failure and the CI issue. The e2e tests use Playwright separately and aren't affected.

Since the fix is just a deletion, I'll give the exact path and git command rather than a full file. The user's concern about duplicate filenames across directories is ironically the exact problem here — app.spec.ts exists in both e2e/ and src/app/, and removing the one in src/app/ resolves it. The real safeguard would be renaming Playwright specs to use .e2e.ts instead of .spec.ts so they can't accidentally collide with Vitest specs in src, but that's a larger refactor than what's needed here. I should focus on the minimal fix—just deleting the misplaced file—and mention the naming convention as an optional improvement. The user mentioned tests are failing when running update.sh locally. The script has || true on the test step so it won't abort, but the failure gets logged and shows up in the output. Removing this duplicate file will fix that.

Show less
Done
The failure isn't a code bug — it's a duplicate file in the wrong directory, which is exactly the "same filename in multiple places" hazard you flagged.

Diagnosis
src/app/app.spec.ts is a Playwright e2e test (it imports test/expect from @playwright/test and uses the page fixture). It is byte-for-byte identical to e2e/app.spec.ts — same SHA-256 (8303df0a…), same 6 tests.

Your unit-test runner discovers specs through tsconfig.spec.json:

json
"include": ["src/**/*.spec.ts", "src/**/*.d.ts"]
So @angular/build:unit-test (Vitest) pulls in src/app/app.spec.ts, hits Playwright's test(), and Playwright throws because it's running outside its own runner. That's the entire error.

e2e/app.spec.ts is the correct home for that test — playwright.config.ts already scopes Playwright with testDir: './e2e', and your real root-component unit test (src/app/app.component.spec.ts, using TestBed + vi.fn()) is well-formed and stays.

The fix: delete one file (no edits needed)
There is no file to rewrite here — the configs are all correct. The only action is removing the stray tracked duplicate:

bash
git rm src/app/app.spec.ts
git commit -m "remove stray Playwright e2e spec from src/ (duplicate of e2e/app.spec.ts; broke Vitest unit run)"
File to delete (exact path): src/app/app.spec.ts
File to keep (do NOT touch): e2e/app.spec.ts — identical content, correct location.

That's it. Nothing in update.sh re-creates it, so once it's gone and pushed it stays gone.

This also fixes GitHub
The CI test-unit job in .github/workflows/test.yml runs yarn ng test --watch=false --coverage — the same discovery path, same collision. Deleting the file fixes the local update.sh run and the GitHub test-unit job in one commit. The e2e job (npx playwright test) was always fine since it reads only ./e2e.

Optional hardening (only if you want belt-and-suspenders)
The structural risk is that any file ending in .spec.ts is ambiguous: Vitest and Playwright both claim it, and only the directory keeps them apart. If you'd rather make a future stray copy impossible to mis-run, switch e2e specs to a distinct suffix — rename e2e/app.spec.ts → e2e/app.e2e.ts and add testMatch: '**/*.e2e.ts' to playwright.config.ts. Then .spec.ts means Vitest, .e2e.ts means Playwright, full stop. Not required to fix today's failure — say the word and I'll hand you the full updated playwright.config.ts and the rename.

Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.
